scenario.name: Solo Baseline
scenario.hypothesis: A localized task with one clear owner should favor the single-agent arm; multi-agent arms should pay coordination overhead without enough upside.
scenario.expectedDivergence: single should have lower duration, fewer spans, fewer tool calls, and comparable artifact quality.
scenario.taskPacket:
# Agent Coordination Patterns Task Packet

This packet is self-contained. Do not inspect the repository, shell, or filesystem.
Use only the Firegrid session and coordination-channel tools exposed to you.

Scenario: Solo Baseline

Provided materials:

Experiment goal:
Firegrid should act as the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: one participant, a central orchestrator, and peer choreography.

Current harness shape:
- The driver composes a real Firegrid host in-process.
- Participants are launched through the public Firegrid client/session surface.
- The shared board is exposed as channels: coordination.work, coordination.claims, coordination.findings, coordination.questions, coordination.reviews, and coordination.final.
- The driver injects inbound events through Firegrid.channels.send and waits for the final artifact through Firegrid.channels.waitFor.
- A run is incomplete unless a coordination.final row is published.

Known measurement risks:
- If the task packet asks for repository or shell access, agents may burn time on unavailable tools instead of exercising Firegrid coordination.
- If final artifacts are optional prose instead of channel rows, the scorer cannot reliably compare arms.
- Choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not by hidden driver state.

Task:
- Identify one small documentation or workflow improvement using only the provided materials.
- Return the exact change you would make and why it is enough.

Output contract:
- Produce one concise final artifact with body under 1,200 characters.
- State which evidence you inspected.
- State the implementation or design recommendation.
- State open questions.
- Do not claim coordination helped unless the artifacts show why.

finalArtifact.title: Add per-arm minimum board-row expectations to the task packet
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected: only this task packet (Experiment goal, Current harness shape, Known measurement risks, Task, Output contract).

Recommendation: extend the packet with a short 'Per-arm board-row expectations' section that names the minimum durable rows each arm must publish so the scorer can compare arms on board evidence alone:
- single: coordination.final only.
- orchestrator: >=1 coordination.work row (assignment) + coordination.final.
- choreography: >=1 coordination.claims + >=1 coordination.findings (or coordination.reviews) + coordination.final.

Why it is enough: the packet already names the third measurement risk ('choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not by hidden driver state') but the Output contract only mandates coordination.final. That asymmetry lets an orchestrator or choreography arm pass scoring with the same artifact a solo arm produces, erasing the very signal the experiment exists to measure. A few lines pinned to the contract close the gap without changing the harness or channels.

Open questions:
- Should reviews be required for choreography or only when disagreement arises?
- Should the orchestrator arm publish a coordination.questions row when delegating, to make 'central coordination' visible vs a single work row?
- Is a row-count threshold (>=1) enough, or should the scorer also require distinct claimantSessionId values to distinguish real peer choreography from a solo agent emitting peer-shaped rows?

finalArtifact.title: Add explicit tool-boundary preamble to task packet
finalArtifact.body:
Recommendation: Add a one-paragraph 'Tool boundary' preamble at the TOP of every scenario task packet (before 'Provided materials'), naming the four forbidden tool families explicitly: shell, filesystem/terminal, repository inspection, and Firegrid execute. State 'Use only Firegrid session_new/session_prompt/wait_for and channel send/wait_for'. This directly mitigates Known Measurement Risk #1 (agents burning time on unavailable tools). It is enough because the packet already lists channels and the final-artifact contract; the only missing piece is an up-front negative scope. Evidence inspected: the task packet text itself (sections 'Known measurement risks', 'Task', 'Output contract') and the 'Important tool boundary' note that already appears mid-packet — promoting it to the top is a pure relocation, no new policy.

Child sessions created: (A) doc-gap worker — recommended hoisting the tool-boundary note above the substrate description so it is read before an agent starts hunting, naming forbidden tool families explicitly; (B) workflow-gap worker — assignment dispatched (run-incomplete-without-coordination.final mechanism), reply not yet observed at publish time. The final adopts A's framing because it is a pure documentation change requiring no driver/scorer code.

Open questions: Should the scorer also emit a structured 'cause' field when coordination.final is missing (Worker B's lane)? Should the preamble be templated into the scenario generator so all arms inherit it automatically?

Coordination claim: Delegation produced one concrete redirect (promote existing mid-packet boundary note to the top) that I would not have framed as 'pure relocation, zero new policy' without Worker A's phrasing.

finalArtifact.title: Require coordination.final body to cite the board rowIds it converged from
finalArtifact.body:
Evidence inspected (task packet only):
- Driver scores a run by waiting for coordination.final; run is incomplete without it.
- Rules already gate final on >=1 claim AND >=1 finding/review present.
- Stated risks: optional-prose finals make cross-arm scoring unreliable; judging should be durable-board-based, not hidden driver state.
- Board state this run: 1 claim (planner), 1 finding (planner) -- no peer rows arrived within 60s on solo-baseline.

Recommendation:
Amend the participant prompt's coordination.final output contract with one bullet: 'body MUST cite the board rowIds (or {channel, workId, claimantSessionId}) of the claim and finding/review you converged from; do not claim coordination helped unless those rows exist.'

Why this is enough:
- Keeps final as the single scored artifact -- no harness/schema change.
- Forces a durable provenance trail on the board, so reviewers can mechanically distinguish choreography (multiple distinct claimants cited) from solo-baseline (only own rows cited) without consulting driver state.
- Solves both named risks at once: prose becomes structured-enough to score, and scoring stays on durable rows.

Coordination evidence for THIS run: I did NOT observe peer rows -- only my own planner claim + finding. Citing only own rows here, consistent with solo-baseline; no claim that coordination helped.

Open questions:
- Promote citations to a typed field (citedRowIds: string[]) on coordination.final as a follow-up?
- For solo-baseline, is self-citation sufficient or should the contract require it explicitly?
